newsSinceJun6: May 2026 monthly revenue (released ~Jun 9 Taiwan time): NT$311.5B, +94.4% YoY but -8.4% MoM, first sequential dip after the record Q1 ramp; Jan-May cumulative NT$1.46T, +82.6% YoY. Consistent with guidance (AI server revenue +132% YoY to ~NT$2.5T in 2026, Q2 AI server sales up double digits QoQ). Separately, a June S&P Global Market Intelligence research note flagged a 'sharp revenue inflection' (total revenue +74% to NT$3.7T in 2026), reinforcing that the AI-server ODM story is now fully consensus. No new contracts, M&A, or guidance changes since Jun 6. Day move -0.4% - quiet tape.

sources.label: Statement Dog - Quanta (2382) monthly revenue incl. May 2026 (NT$311.5B, +94.4% YoY, -8.4% MoM)
S&P Global Market Intelligence (June 2026) - AI server boom to drive sharp revenue inflection for Quanta
Investing.com - Quanta sees AI server sales growing triple digits in 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 16: May revenue (+94% YoY) confirms execution but adds nothing on mispricing, while a fresh June S&P Global inflection note and broker target hikes show the ODM story is fully consensus - residual alpha drifts slightly lower on this crowding-sensitive page.
verdicts.bull: AI server revenue tracking toward +132% YoY with capacity doubling in Thailand/US by end-2026; any GB-rack allocation upside or general-server upcycle extends the beat-raise cadence.
verdicts.neutral: Numbers are excellent but exactly what guidance promised; at +35% YTD and ~$48B cap the market already pays for triple-digit AI server growth, leaving rank 16 about right.
verdicts.bear: First MoM revenue decline (-8.4%) since the Q1 ramp plus 4.8% gross margin shows the AI mix is dilutive; a hyperscaler capex digestion pause hits a now-crowded long hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results confirming double-digit QoQ AI server growth and stabilizing gross margin as Thailand/US capacity ramps.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Margin compression from AI mix and ODM pricing pressure, or a hyperscaler order digestion phase, unwinding the consensus rerating.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 58: on the deployment/systems-integration lens, May revenue +94% YoY and Jan-May +82.6% YoY are direct confirmation that rack-integration capacity is converting to shipments - incremental positive drift, not a rank-changing event.
verdicts.bull: Server mix now ~84% of revenue with AI ~80% of that; doubling AI rack capacity by end-2026 makes Quanta one of the few at-scale physical deployment chokepoints for hyperscaler buildouts.
verdicts.neutral: Execution is on plan and already audited into the current rank; the -8.4% MoM dip is monthly noise within guided double-digit QoQ Q2 growth.
verdicts.bear: Razor-thin margins mean deployment scale does not translate to earnings power; competitor ODMs (Foxconn, Wiwynn) adding capacity could erode allocation share and pricing.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June/Q2 revenue prints validating the guided sequential AI-server acceleration into 2H26.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI server ASP/margin pressure and customer concentration in a handful of hyperscalers.
